FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Struct RepeatLayout

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A pluggable layout for ItemsRepeater(int, Func<int, Element>, in RepeatLayout, Func<int, string>?, int, Action<int>?, Action<int>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<int, int>?, ItemCollectionTransition?) — the equivalent of WinUI's Layout object. A readonly struct (no per-call allocation). Stack/Grid/Custom virtualize (only the window is realized); Wrap/Inline are non-virtual (build the whole child list — use for SMALL collections like a nav pane or a toolbar). Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/HorizontalGrid ride the Custom slot (every layout object IS an IVirtualLayout; variable-extent ones are IMeasuredVirtualLayout — E11-L0's one seam). NOTE: the stateful layouts (Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/Grouped) own realize tables — when the OWNING component re-renders, hoist the layout instance (e.g. UseMemo) instead of calling the factory inline each render.

public readonly struct RepeatLayout

Horizontally-scrolling uniform grid (a shelf rows cells tall), by column.

Non-virtual stack (column/row) — for small collections (nav panes, toolbars).

RepeatLayout

LinedFlow(float, Func<int, float>?, float, float)

The WinUI LinedFlowLayout photo-wall (uniform-height lines, aspect-ratio widths). Stateful — hoist when the owner re-renders (see the struct remarks).

public static RepeatLayout LinedFlow(float lineHeight, Func<int, float>? aspectRatio = null, float lineSpacing = 0, float minItemSpacing = 0)

Parameters

lineHeight float
aspectRatio Func<int, float>
lineSpacing float
minItemSpacing float

Returns

RepeatLayout

Measured(IMeasuredVirtualLayout, bool)

Virtualized with a variable-extent IMeasuredVirtualLayout (estimate-then-correct + anchoring).

layout IMeasuredVirtualLayout
horizontal bool

RepeatLayout

SpanGrid(int, float, float, Func<int, int>)

Uniform-row grid with item spanning (hero rows). Stateful — hoist when the owner re-renders.

RepeatLayout
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
